channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: 'Premflix' and tourist fans - future of football predictions
item.description: Former broadcasting executive Neil Duncanson and author Alex Fynn, who made remarkably accurate predictions about football's future in 1994, discuss what the game will look like in 10 years' time.

item.title: Unacceptable season cannot be repeated - Van Dijk
item.description: Liverpool captain Virgil van Dijk says his side's season has been "unacceptable" following an 18th defeat in all competitions.

item.title: What happened to Everton's £40m signing Dibling?
item.description: The curious case of Tyler Dibling - BBC Sport chief football writers asks what has happened to Everton's £40m youngster and what does the future hold?

item.title: Has tide turned for troubled Spurs under De Zerbi?
item.description: Is Tottenham Hotspur's superb win at Aston Villa the moment the tide turned in their fight against Premier League relegation, asks chief football writer Phil McNulty?

item.title: Carrick has the Ferguson magic, says Cunha
item.description: Manchester United forward Matheus Cunha believes interim boss Michael Carrick is the perfect man to reconnect the club to its trophy-laden past.

item.title: A huge night for Spurs - but who are favourites to go down?
item.description: Tottenham's 2-1 win at Aston Villa moved them out of the Premier League relegation zone - but who are now the favourites to go down?

item.title: Man Utd beat Liverpool to secure Champions League place
item.description: Watch highlights as Manchester United secure their spot in the Champions League next season after a 3-2 win over Liverpool in the Premier League.

item.title: Relief for Celtic... now does O'Neill want Rangers to win?
item.description: Celtic interim manager Martin O'Neill recognises there is still "little margin for error" after his side beat 10-man Hibernian to draw level with Hearts at the top of the Scottish Premiership.

item.title: Quiz: Name every Man Utd goalscorer against Liverpool since 2010
item.description: From 2010 to the present day, there have been 23 players who have scored for Manchester United against Liverpool in the Premier League. Can you name them all in our quiz?

item.title: Spurs out of relegation zone with Villa win
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur propel themselves out of the relegation zone with a commanding 2-1 win over Aston Villa in the Premier League.

item.title: Manchester City snatch late win over Liverpool to extend WSL title lead
item.description: Watch highlights as Manchester City's Rebecca Knaak scores a late winner against Liverpool to extend their title lead in the Women's Super League.

item.title: Vinicius scores twice as Madrid win to keep Barcelona waiting for title
item.description: Vinicius Jr scores twice as Real Madrid beat Espanyol 2-0 to keep Barcelona waiting to win the La Liga title ahead of next week's El Clasico.

item.title: Bournemouth up to sixth with win over poor Palace
item.description: Bournemouth move up to sixth place to boost their hopes of finishing in a European spot after beating Crystal Palace 3-0 at the Vitality Stadium.

item.title: Carrick 'deserves chance' to continue at Man Utd - Shearer
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Alan Shearer says Michael Carrick "deserves a chance" as Manchester United's manager next season after securing a Champions League spot.

item.title: Davide Ancelotti: My dad, my tactics and Brazil's World Cup prospects
item.description: Brazil assistant manager Davide Ancelotti sits down with BBC Sport's tactics expert Umir Irfan to discuss his coaching philosophy, inspirations and ideals.

item.title: Manager Marcelino to leave Villarreal
item.description: Manager Marcelino will leave Villarreal at the end of the season after three years in charge.

item.title: Hearts trio pick up PFA Scotland prizes
item.description: Claudio Braga is the is the PFA Scotland Premiership Player of the Year, with Derek McInnes and Eva Olid making it a managerial double for the Tynecastle club.

item.title: North Korean side to make rare trip to South Korea
item.description: A team of North Korean footballers will make a rare visit to South Korea later this month.

item.title: The battle for sixth... and why teams might need to lose on final day
item.description: As the Premier League's battle for European places hots up, BBC Sport assesses the situation - and some very strange potential quirks in the race.

item.title: Slot's VAR fury but loss highlights Liverpool issues
item.description: Liverpool manager Arne Slot was furious that Manchester United's second goal was allowed, but also highlighted issues with his own side after the 3-2 defeat.

item.title: Ferguson taken to hospital as precaution before Man Utd v Liverpool
item.description: Sir Alex Ferguson is taken to hospital after falling unwell at Old Trafford shortly before Manchester United's Premier League match with Liverpool on Sunday.

item.title: Juventus target Man City's Reijnders - Monday's gossip
item.description: Juventus target Man City's Tijjani Reijnders, Newcastle hope to retain striker Will Osula, Crystal Palace and Chelsea eye Bournemouth boss Andoni Iraola, plus more.

item.title: What would it cost England and Scotland fans to go to World Cup?
item.description: High ticket prices, expensive accommodation and train fares way above normal rates. If you want to book a World Cup trip now, this is what it would cost.

item.title: Should Sesko goal have been ruled out?
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Alan Shearer and Micah Richards believe Benjamin Sesko's goal against Liverpool should not have stood after it brushed his fingers, saying under the laws of the game it should have been a handball.

item.title: 'Exceptional' Mainoo is proving his worth - Richards
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Micah Richards says Manchester United's Kobbie Mainoo is "exceptional" after scoring the winning goal in their match against Liverpool in the Premier League.

item.title: How 'outstanding' Tottenham beat Villa
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Micah Richards analyses Tottenham Hotspur's "brave" press during their win against Aston Villa in the Premier League.

item.title: Spurs 'cannot be happy' despite Villa win - De Zerbi
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur boss Roberto De Zerbi says his players "cannot be happy yet" after they moved out of the relegation zone after beating Aston Villa 2-1 at Villa Park.

item.title: Chelsea beat Leicester City to secure Champions League place - highlights
item.description: Watch highlights as Chelsea secure a spot in the Women's Champions League after beating Leicester City 3-1 in the Women's Super League.

item.title: Carrick secures Champions League - what are Man Utd waiting for?
item.description: Unless Manchester United appoint Luis Enrique, it is hard to think of an acceptable alternative to giving Michael Carrick the job on a permanent basis, writes Simon Stone.

item.title: Forget Spurs... relegated Barrow are kings of chaos
item.description: After five managers during the season, 'Kings of chaos' Barrow have been relegated from League Two.

item.title: Watford sack boss Still after less than three months
item.description: Watford sack head coach Ed Still after less than three months in charge at Vicarage Road following the team's failure to reach the play-offs.

item.title: Has much-maligned Gyokeres proved his critics wrong?
item.description: The talk before the season was that Arsenal needed a 20-goal-a-season striker to win a trophy - and now they finally have one in Viktor Gyokeres.

item.title: Credit for McKenna and Hull a play-off force - Sam Parkin's EFL takeaways
item.description: BBC Final Score Sam Parkin gives his take on the big EFL talking points as Ipswich win promotion and Hull make the play-offs.

item.title: Holders Rangers beat Montrose to set up Old Firm cup final
item.description: Holders Rangers make it to their fourth consecutive Women's Scottish Cup final - where they will face rivals Celtic - after a 2-0 victory over brave Montrose at Hampden.

item.title: 'I've never been part of a game like it' - how the play-off shootout unfolded
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at how the three-way race for the final Championship play-off spot unfolded between Hull City, Wrexham and Derby County.

item.title: Is it advantage Arsenal in dramatic title race?
item.description: Are Arsenal now firmly in pole position to be Premier League champions after the Gunners went six points clear of Manchester City with a 3-0 win over Fulham.

item.title: 'Shy' Anderson on England nerves and becoming a leader
item.description: Nottingham Forest midfielder Elliot Anderson tells Kelly Somers about coming out of his shell to become a leader.

item.title: 'We got police escort to Vardy's party' - Leicester's 5,000-1 title win 10 years on
item.description: Premier League title-winning captain Wes Morgan and former Leicester City team-mate Marc Albrighton reflect on the club's 5,000-1 triumph a decade on.

item.title: 'I lost myself' - Bright on retirement, abuse and the future
item.description: Former England and Chelsea captain Millie Bright speaks to BBC Sport a day after announcing her retirement.

item.title: Making history and facing Neymar - Lingard on life in Brazil
item.description: In his first major interview since moving to Brazil, Jesse Lingard talks to the BBC about his new life, and Manchester United.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Hearts chase history as Rangers reach critical point
item.description: "Tynecastle will be an electrifying experience," writes Tom English, previewing Rangers' visit to Scottish Premiership pacesetters Heart of Midlothian.

item.title: Why are McBurnie and Stewart not in World Cup mix?
item.description: Oli McBurnie and Ross Stewart are arguably Scotland's two most in-form strikers, yet neither appear to be in Steve Clarke's World Cup plans, writes Tom English.

item.title: Play-off pressure piles up for St Mirren v Kilmarnock
item.description: Kilmarnock's trip to visit St Mirren on Saturday could potentially decide which of the sides is consigned to a play-off to avoid relegation from the Scottish Premiership.

item.title: Watch: SPFL round-up as Inverness CT & East Kilbride celebrate
item.description: All the goals from the final weekend in the Championship, League 1 and League 2 as Inverness CT and East Kilbride celebrate title victories.

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
